title: The Francis Ford Coppola Hall of Fame and ‘Megalopolis’
format: Sean and Amanda pan Coppola's self-financed 'Megalopolis' after a chaotic IMAX screening, then walk his whole filmography to build a Hall of Fame, inducting eleven films, from 'Patton' to the folly they just watched.

segments.heading: 'Megalopolis'
segments.note: the 40-year passion project, finally in theaters

films.title: Megalopolis
films.note: Coppola self-financed this to the tune of a reported $120 million, selling off part of his wine business. Adam Driver plays architect Caesar Catalina, who can stop time and wants to rebuild New Rome with a wonder-material called Megalon. Both hosts respect the ambition and think the execution fails: Sean compares the all-digital artifice to 'The Phantom Menace,' and their bro-heavy IMAX screening, opened by a rambling live Coppola/De Niro/Spike Lee Q&A, tipped into open laughter. Only Adam Driver and, arguably, Shia LaBeouf and Aubrey Plaza seem to be in the movie it should have been.

segments.heading: The Francis Ford Coppola Hall of Fame
segments.note: the greens: eleven films (they cheated to eleven)

films.title: Patton
films.note: The screenplay Oscar that bumped his quote and made him a Hollywood player. Eligible as a writing credit; the only one of his four to make the cut.

films.title: The Godfather
films.note: An airport novel made grand and poetic. Sean's pick for the most fun of his films to watch.

films.title: The Conversation
films.note: Gene Hackman as surveillance man Harry Caul; a prescient film about listening and being sure you know what you don't.

films.title: The Godfather Part II
films.note: The twin peak. Amanda re-listened to the Rewatchables on it rather than rewatch; she and Bill Simmons still disagree about Kay.

films.title: Apocalypse Now
films.note: The near-fatal jungle shoot that came out great anyway. Sean calls a 70mm screening 'church.'

films.title: One from the Heart
films.note: The Las Vegas soundstage musical that bombed him into debt: $26M spent, $600K returned. Beautiful, and a clear ancestor of 'Megalopolis.' The Reprise recut trims about 15 minutes.

films.title: The Outsiders
films.note: Inducted as a slash with 'Rumble Fish.' The S.E. Hinton adaptation with the young cast that got famous: Cruise, Swayze, Lowe, Dillon, Macchio.

films.title: Rumble Fish
films.note: The arthouse companion to 'The Outsiders': black-and-white, German-expressionist, the one that's grown more celebrated.

films.title: Tucker: The Man and His Dream
films.note: The most one-to-one 'I am Tucker' movie, and the clearest key to Coppola's belief in the visionary who sees the future first. Lucas helped get it made.

films.title: Bram Stoker's Dracula
films.note: Sean's strong-arm pick. Uneven performances (sorry, Keanu), but Gary Oldman is 'wowzers good.'

films.title: Megalopolis
films.note: In for calling his shot and as the summation of his life's project, despite both hosts agreeing it doesn't work.

segments.heading: Weighed but cut
segments.note: the yellows and reds from the filmography walk

films.title: Dementia 13
films.note: The Roger Corman-produced $3 horror starter. Better than expected; yellowed.

films.title: You're a Big Boy Now
films.note: One of the few Coppola films Sean really doesn't like: dated, antic, Austin Powers-y in black and white.

films.title: Finian's Rainbow
films.note: The Fred Astaire studio musical at the death of Old Hollywood. Stinks; red.

films.title: The Rain People
films.note: His New Hollywood road movie with James Caan; the sensitive-woman's-picture of his career. A respectful 'marigold yellow.'

films.title: The Cotton Club
films.note: Dull and artificial: men in suspenders arguing, then maybe shooting. Red.

films.title: Captain EO
films.note: The 17-minute Michael Jackson 3-D attraction film for Disney parks. A yellow, as an experiment and a paycheck.

films.title: Peggy Sue Got Married
films.note: Kathleen Turner and weird late-'80s Nic Cage (Coppola's nephew). Solidly good, but a fairly impersonal for-hire high-concept; not in.

films.title: Gardens of Stone
films.note: A quiet, devastating drama made as his son Gio died in production. Maybe his most obscure film; red.

films.title: New York Stories
films.note: His 'Life Without Zoe' segment, with daughter Sofia. Not going in.

films.title: The Godfather Part III
films.note: A commercially successful misfire (and Pacino's hair). Red.

films.title: Jack
films.note: Robin Williams as a boy aging four times as fast. Pilloried; 'I wanted to make it,' Coppola said. Red.

films.title: The Rainmaker
films.note: The John Grisham adaptation with a pre-'Good Will Hunting' Matt Damon. An 'I'm glad it exists' canary yellow.

films.title: Youth Without Youth
films.note: Tim Roth as a man thrown back 30 years; part of his 'studies' for 'Megalopolis.' The most successful of his late reflective trio; yellow.

films.title: Tetro
films.note: Black-and-white Buenos Aires family drama with Vincent Gallo and Alden Ehrenreich. Good, not great; out.

films.title: Twixt
films.note: Val Kilmer as an author probing small-town murders. Tonally the closest to 'Megalopolis'; out.
